vulnerabilities.description: No input validation. User-supplied values stored to JSON without sanitization.
Memory files created with world-readable permissions (0o644). Exposes secrets to other local users.
Temp file world-readable before gh execution. Race condition exposes PR/issue bodies.
Session files lack permission restrictions. Default 0o644 readable by other users.
dangerouslySetInnerHTML used for static CSS. Use CSS modules instead.

recommendations: Fix memory.js: Add input validation + set file permissions to 0o600 with fs.chmodSync()
Fix session.js: Set file permissions to 0o600 after creation
Fix github-safe.js: Create temp files with 0o600 mode flag
Run npm audit to identify dependency CVEs
Add pre-commit hooks (detect-secrets) to prevent secret commits
Set Content-Security-Policy headers if serving via Express
Document .env in .gitignore; validate env vars at startup

rawOutputPreview:
Security audit complete. Key findings:

**Critical Issues:**
1. **memory.js (HIGH)** — No input validation on stored values; sensitive data stored world-readable
2. **File permissions (HIGH)** — Session & memory files default to 0o644/0o777; should be 0o600

**Medium Issues:**
1. **github-safe.js** — Temp file race condition; create with 0o600 mode
2. **dangerouslySetInnerHTML** — Use CSS modules instead (Workers.tsx:125)

**Low Issues:**
1. Audit dependencies (express, @google/genai)
2. Pre-commit hooks needed to prevent secret commits
